section.p: Diagnose IPsec connection failures caused by firewall-related problems.
If a firewall on an endpoint or an intermediate route drops Internet Key Exchange version 2 (IKEv2) packets, the ipsec auto --up command times out and shows repeated retransmission messages:
section.codeblock: 181 "vpn.example.com"[1] 192.0.2.2 #15: initiating IKEv2 IKE SA 181 "vpn.example.com"[1] 192.0.2.2 #15: STATE_PARENT_I1: sent v2I1, expected v2R1 010 "vpn.example.com"[1] 192.0.2.2 #15: STATE_PARENT_I1: retransmission; will wait 0.5 seconds for response 010 "vpn.example.com"[1] 192.0.2.2 #15: STATE_PARENT_I1: retransmission; will wait 1 seconds for response 010 "vpn.example.com"[1] 192.0.2.2 #15: STATE_PARENT_I1: retransmission; will wait 2 seconds for response ...
section.p: You can use the tcpdump utility to capture traffic and verify if IKE or IPsec packets are dropped by a firewall, for example:
section.codeblock: # tcpdump -i <interface> -n -n esp or udp port 500 or udp port 4500 or tcp port 4500
section.p: Note that tcpdump is of limited use for diagnosing other types of IPsec problems because the IKE protocol is encrypted.
